template: You are an expert QA/QC engineer responsible for preparing a comprehensive Inspection and Testing Plan (ITP) for {{work_title}}, related to the {{discipline}}, covering inspection stages: {{inspection_stage}}, involving stakeholders: {{stakeholders}}, and applying QA/QC roles: {{roles}}, in accordance with reference standards: {{ref_standards}}, and considering special requirements, constraints, or remarks provided in: {{remarks}}; you must generate a complete, structured, and professional ITP in tabular format suitable for construction quality management and project execution control systems. The ITP shall decompose {{work_title}} into logically sequenced inspection, testing, verification, and documentation activities aligned with the {{discipline}} scope of work, systematically organized under relevant stages from {{inspection_stage}}, ensuring full lifecycle coverage including material receiving, preparation, construction, installation, inspection, testing, pre-commissioning, commissioning, and reporting/documentation review where applicable. For each activity define quantum of check (e.g., 100%, sample basis, random inspection, per lot, per batch, continuous monitoring), applicable reference standards strictly derived from {{ref_standards}} and relevant project/industry specifications, acceptance norms that are clear, measurable, testable, and fully compliant with those standards, and record formats such as Inspection Requests (IR), Material Inspection Requests (MIR), Test Reports, Checklists, NCRs, Punch Lists, Calibration Records, or Commissioning Certificates; assign relevant stakeholders from {{stakeholders}} to each activity and map each stakeholder to exactly one appropriate QA/QC role selected from {{roles}} such as Hold Point, Witness, Inspection, Review, or Surveillance, ensuring role assignment reflects inspection criticality, contractual requirements, and QA/QC hierarchy without duplication or ambiguity. Ensure all activities are non-redundant, logically sequenced according to construction workflow, fully traceable across QA/QC control points, and expressed using precise engineering terminology consistent with industry standards, while ensuring complete lifecycle inspection coverage and practical applicability to real project execution; the final output must be strictly in tabular format only with columns Stage, Activity, Quantum of Check, Reference Standards, Acceptance Norms, Record Formats, {{stakeholders}}, having Role of each, without any additional explanation or text outside the table.
